questions.question: 1. What is the primary purpose of data preprocessing in clustering?
options: B) To prevent large-scale features from dominating distance calculations
C) To change the clustering algorithm type
D) To increase the total number of data point

questions.question: 2. Which scaling method ensures the data has a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1?
3. What is the typical output range of data after applying Min-Max Scaling?
4. Why do algorithms like K-Means perform poorly on unscaled data?
options: A) They rely on absolute numerical differences
B) They can only process integer values
C) They automatically ignore large numbers

questions.question: 5. Which scaling technique is the most resistant to extreme outliers?
6. How do severe outliers negatively impact Min-Max Scaling?
options: A) They change the standard deviation of the dataset
B) They squash the normal data into a tiny, compressed range
C) They shift the median value significantly
D) They force all values to become negative

questions.question: 7. What statistical measures are utilized by Robust Scaling?
8. In an unscaled dataset containing Age (18–80) and Income ($30,000–$150,000), how will K-Means likely behave?
options: A) It will weight Age more heavily
B) It will weight both features equally
C) It will group points primarily based on Income
D) It will fail to calculate distances

questions.question: 9. Why does Standard Scaling struggle when a dataset contains severe outliers?
options: A) Outliers artificially inflate the standard deviation and shift the mean
B) Outliers cannot be converted into Z-scores
C) Outliers drastically change the IQR
D) Outliers force the maximum value to become 1

questions.question: 10. Which of the following formulas correctly represents Min-Max scaling?
